title: Intermittent Wrong Gateway on the Engineering Lab VLAN

ticket.summary: Several (not all) laptops in the Engineering Lab (VLAN 120, 10.60.10.0/24) intermittently lose internet access and show a default gateway of 192.168.1.1 instead of the normal 10.60.10.1. Affected users say a reboot or 'ipconfig /renew' sometimes fixes it, sometimes makes it worse. Started yesterday afternoon; roughly 1 in 4 new connections are affected.

topology.description: The Engineering Lab (VLAN 120, 10.60.10.0/24) is served by the corporate DHCP server dhcp-01 (10.1.1.5), which hands out gateway 10.60.10.1 and DNS 10.0.0.60. The lab has open, unmanaged access ports on switch sw-lab-01 that engineers frequently patch personal equipment into for testing. Port Gi1/0/14 was recently used by an engineer testing a consumer-grade travel router (TP-Link model, factory defaults) that was left plugged in and powered on overnight.

Jul 13 14:02:14 %DHCP_SNOOPING-5-DHCP_SNOOPING_UNTRUSTED_PORT: DHCP packet (type OFFER) received from untrusted port GigabitEthernet1/0/14, source 192.168.1.1
Jul 13 14:03:42 %DHCP_SNOOPING-5-DHCP_SNOOPING_UNTRUSTED_PORT: DHCP packet (type OFFER) received from untrusted port GigabitEthernet1/0/14, source 192.168.1.1

Note: OUI b0:4e:26 registers to TP-Link Corporation.

monitoring.notes: The DHCP offer-source monitor (fed from DHCP snooping logs) shows VLAN 120 normally sees 100% of offers sourced from 10.1.1.5. Starting yesterday at 13:58, roughly 20-25% of offers are being logged as sourced from 192.168.1.1 on port Gi1/0/14 -- a source address that isn't even in the 10.60.10.0/24 subnet, meaning any client that races that offer and wins gets an unusable gateway/DNS.

hints: Not every client is affected, and the ones that are get an IP outside the 10.60.10.0/24 range entirely -- that points to two DHCP servers racing, not a scope or relay problem.
Check the DHCP snooping / offer-source logs on sw-lab-01 for VLAN 120 to see where offers are actually coming from.

A consumer router (TP-Link OUI b0:4e:26) plugged into unmanaged port Gi1/0/14 is handing out its factory-default 192.168.1.0/24 addresses and racing the real DHCP server -- DHCP snooping is enabled globally on the switch but was never applied to VLAN 120, so the rogue offers aren't being blocked.

solution.root_cause: A consumer-grade router with factory-default settings (TP-Link, MAC OUI b0:4e:26) was plugged into unmanaged access port Gi1/0/14 on sw-lab-01 and is acting as a rogue DHCP server, handing out 192.168.1.0/24 addresses and gateway/DNS that are unreachable from the corporate network. It intermittently wins the DHCP race against the legitimate dhcp-01 (10.1.1.5) because DHCP snooping is enabled globally on the switch but was never activated on VLAN 120, so the untrusted port's offers aren't being filtered.
solution.fix: Disconnect the rogue device from Gi1/0/14, then enable DHCP snooping on VLAN 120 (ip dhcp snooping vlan 120) with only the uplink to dhcp-01 configured as a trusted port, so future rogue offers on access ports are dropped automatically.
acceptable_answers: dhcp snooping not enabled on vlan
